submission.questionnaireText:
William Linton
Adult 04/02/1984 (41)  Schedule appointment  Edit

1. Initial Health Survey (complete at your earliest convenience)
08/11/2025 at 4:35 PM
Note



1. Do you gain weight easily?
(1) I do not experience this
2. [IF SO] Where do you typically gain weight on your body?
Not Applicable
3. Do you feel "puffy," i.e your fat tends to be soft and feel like water retention?
(1) I do not experience this
4. Do you struggle with unintended weight loss?
(4) This is a very bothersome issue for me
5. [WOMEN ONLY] Do you accumulate cellulite?
Not Applicable
6. Do you struggle with low appetite?
(3) This is a fairly bothersome issue for me
7. Do you tend to wake up when sleeping?
(2) This is a somewhat bothersome issue for me
8. If you tend to wake up when sleeping, is it: within the first few hours of falling asleep OR later on in the night OR both?
Both
9. If you tend to wake up when sleeping, is it to: urinate OR eat something OR drink something OR seemingly no reason?
Urinate
10. If you tend to wake up when sleeping, do you find that you are hot or sweaty upon waking?
Yes
11. Do you NOT dream?
(1) I do not experience this
12. Do you experience nightmares or sleep paralysis?
(2) This is a somewhat bothersome issue for me
13. Do you have trouble falling asleep?
(3) This is a fairly bothersome issue for me
14. Do you feel like you DON'T get a restful sleep?
(4) This is a very bothersome issue for me
15. Do you frequently feel cold, especially at the extremities (fingertips, toes, and nose)?
(1) I do not experience this
16. [WOMEN ONLY] Do you frequently have painful menstrual cramps?
Not Applicable
17. [WOMEN ONLY] Do you frequently have irregular menstruation or suffer from premenstrual syndrome?
Not Applicable
18. Do you suffer from chronic congestion without mucus?
(1) I do not experience this
19. Do you frequently experience mucus buildup in the throat or nose?
(3) This is a fairly bothersome issue for me
20. [IF SO] Is it exacerbated after eating?
Yes
21. Do you suffer from dental problems such as bad breath, cavities, and gum inflammation?
(2) This is a somewhat bothersome issue for me
22. Do you often find that you are irritable or easily agitated?
(3) This is a fairly bothersome issue for me
23. Do you suffer from "brain fog," low energy or feel like you do not have mental clarity?
(4) This is a very bothersome issue for me
24. Do you tend to have an "afternoon crash" after eating lunch?
(3) This is a fairly bothersome issue for me
25. Do you often feel "hyper aware," jittery, or restless?
(3) This is a fairly bothersome issue for me
26. Do you notice the sensation of your heart pounding or difficulty breathing?
(2) This is a somewhat bothersome issue for me
27. Have you had a loss of pleasure in activities you normally enjoy?
(4) This is a very bothersome issue for me
28. Have you noticed a lack of sexual interest or desire?
(2) This is a somewhat bothersome issue for me
29. [MEN ONLY] Have you lost morning erections?
(1) I do not experience this
30. Do you have skin issues such as dry skin, eczema, or acne?
(4) This is a very bothersome issue for me
31. Can you trace a worsening of your symptoms back to a traumatic event?
Yes
32. Do you have specific food cravings? If so, please list the foods you crave.
Yes
Dark chocolate
Siete Serrano vinegar potato chips
Yogurt/sour cream
33. Do you have a history of mental health issues? If so, please list them.
Yes
Depression, anxiety (neither of them diagnosed)
34. Do you experience constipation?
(2) This is a somewhat bothersome issue for me
35. Do you experience nausea?
(2) This is a somewhat bothersome issue for me
36. Do you experience bloating (subjective feeling)?
(3) This is a fairly bothersome issue for me
37. Do you experience distention (visible expansion of the abdomen)?
(2) This is a somewhat bothersome issue for me
38. Do you experience diarrhea?
(2) This is a somewhat bothersome issue for me
39. Do you experience belching?
(2) This is a somewhat bothersome issue for me
40. Do you experience flatulence?
(2) This is a somewhat bothersome issue for me
41. Do you experience pain in your upper abdomen?
(3) This is a fairly bothersome issue for me
42. Do you experience pain in your right upper quadrant?
(1) I do not experience this
43. Do you experience pain in your lower abdomen?
(2) This is a somewhat bothersome issue for me
44. Are you aware of any specific foods that trigger your symptoms? If so, please list them.
Yes
Bloating, slight: chicken or Amy's French Country soup
Bloating, previously: oatmeal at night, white rice, quinoa, high-fat dinners, frozen pineapple (could have beenjkjkjkjk the cold)
Nausea, previously: high-fat dinners
Drinking or eating very cold things has caused bloating, cramping, diarrhea.
45. Do you experience dry stools?
(2) This is a somewhat bothersome issue for me
46. Do you experience acid reflux?
(4) This is a very bothersome issue for me
47. Do your symptoms worsen IMMEDIATELY after eating?
(2) This is a somewhat bothersome issue for me
48. Do your symptoms worsen 1-3 hours after eating?
(3) This is a fairly bothersome issue for me
49. Do symptoms alleviate after a bowel movement?
Sometimes
50. Have you tried anything in the past to alleviate your symptoms? These can be dietary changes, supplements, prescriptions or anything else. If so, please list them, and how helpful (or unhelpful) they were for you. Please be as specific as possible, including specific foods and brands and dosages of supplements, as well as what symptoms they affected.
Yes
Holistic Nutritionist
In June 2019 due to heartburn I worked with a holistic nutritionist. I did everything listed below at once and immediately noticed that heartburn stopped, stress and brain fog reduced, mood and productivity improved, and unexpectedly a ganglion cyst on top of my left wrist almost completely disappeared within a week (I'd had it for 10 years). I did lose weight though (not a good thing) since it was hard to get enough to eat following his dietary recommendations. This was my first time doing any of these supplements, dietary or lifestyle changes. We did so many things that it's hard to say exactly what helped and what didn't. In September 2019 I reintroduced oatmeal, rice, legumes, and nuts and most other things since I started lifting weights and needed the calories. The ganglion cyst started to come back over time. In October 2020 I reintroduced dairy (later that day I noticed the ganglion cyst was bigger), and in 2021 I reintroduced gluten (the next day the ganglion cyst disappeared completely but a new smaller one appeared on the side of my wrist of the same hand).

Eliminated:
Sugar and high carb foods including all grains
Legumes
Dairy
Gluten
Seed oils
Food dyes, additives
Soy
Pork
Shellfish
Histamine-rich / DAO-blocking: All fermented foods, vinegar, walnuts, cashews, smoked fish, alcohol, chocolate, papaya, pineapple, strawberries, black tea, green tea
Antibacterial/chemical cleaners, personal care products, etc
Fluoride
Did:
At least 100g animal protein and 3 Tbsp of animal fat daily
Reduce EMFs
Reduce blue light
Daily grounding
Used Mother Dirt for skin
Lots of veggies
Drink 75oz of water with trace minerals
Hot/cold shower therapy + Wim hof breathing
Frequent movement/walks
Trace minerals
Supplements:
Carlson Cod Liver oil liquid - 1tsp of liquid daily
RegeneRest (Clinical Synergy) - 2 daily 30-45min before bed.
Betaine HCL - 250mg, 2x daily w/ lunch + dinner
Methylcobalamin Vitamin B12 (Lozenge, sublingual, dissolvable, liquid or similar) - 2000mcg daily Mornings and/or midday
Basic Nutrients 2/Day (Thorne Research) - 1 cap, 2x daily w/ breakfast + lunch
Reishi - 2g, 2x daily
P5P pyridoxal 5' phosphate (activated B6) - 50mg, 2x daily w/ food
Slippery Elm - 800mg, 3x daily w/ meals
Saccharomyces Boulardii - days 7-29, 2 daily w/ lunch
Beta Glucans w/Immune Enhancer (NOW) - days 10-40, 1 daily w/ dinner
Meriva (Curcumin Phytosome) - days 10-30, 500mg, 2x daily w/ lunch + dinner
Chickweed - days 15-33, 25 Drops daily evenings
Note: most of these were only for 30 days, except for cod liver oil which I continued to do once per week, and reishi which I continued to do once daily. I stopped both of them probably around Sept 2019.
This nutritionist also encouraged me not to worry so much about contamination/washing hands, etc which was definitely helpful for stress levels.
——————————–
——————————–
Gaining weight/preventing weight loss:
Adding extra calories with EVOO seems to work but does not sit well with stomach/small intestine
Eating huge Chick Fil A breakfasts in 2016 caused massive water retention on one side of my abdomen so I stopped
Eating lots of protein bars in 2016 - 2018 helped
Lifting weights (barbells + accessory lifts) 3x/week and eating 3500 cal/day and 150 - 170g protein/day with relatively low fat and higher carbs definitely helped (went from 135 lb in Sept 2019 to 165 lb in March 2020) but I was having 5 daily bowel movements of 5 - 6 on the Bristol stool scale. Lots of oatmeal and nuts for breakfast, felt like that contributed to the GI distress.
Neck tension:
Lifting weights and gaining weight helped. Maybe my muscles were more relaxed, but my back muscles were bigger too, which I think allowed for better alignment during sleep. (My head is long back-to-forward which causes my neck to crane when lying down.)
Using a different pillow: This has been key. I tend to fall asleep on my side and wake up on my back. It's hard to find a pillow that's the right height for both positions. I seem to have an okay set up right now and it does help.
Wildman Athletica 3-min neck routine: Good warmup for lifting, but doesn't seem to help with chronic tension.
Upper body mobilization routine: I do this in the mornings and it helps.
Carpal tunnel syndrome/RSI:
Stopping all typing/mousing for 1-2 weeks helps when I have a flare up
Reducing mobile phone usage helps
Walking helps
Chiropractor adjustment and mobilizing/popping the thumb joint helped
PT with visceral manipulation helped very slowly over time
Taking breaks during the day helps (ideally 5-10 min every 20-30 min of typing)
Mobilizing upper body and doing wrist resilience mobilizations in the morning before typing help
Eliminating mouse usage helped (I use trackpads instead)
Moving from a flat keyboard to a tented keyboard helped significantly
Moving from a tented keyboard to a fully split keyboard with the halves vertical helped significantly
Moving my fully split keyboard with the halves vertical to a position lower down so my arms are perpendicular to the ground helped significantly
For Heartburn/acid feeling in stomach/slight pain in stomach area/H. Pylori symptoms:
Holistic Nutritionist program in 2019: It cleared up heartburn
April - August 2023: HCL ProZyme, 1 tab per day - Don't remember whether it helped
July 2024: OBMT (Omeprazole + Bismuth + Metronidazole + Tetracycline) – 4 doses per day prescribed for 14 days. Immediately after each dose I felt strong restlessness and a weird feeling in my head; I was concerned about intracranial hypertension, so I only did 10 days and took two 1-day breaks in the middle (I know you're not supposed to do that). This was hell on my digestion obviously, but I feel like it helped, I had more energy and less heartburn after the treatment. It didn't last though, both issues came back a few months later.
Doing the UltraFlora Balance Probiotic (1 or 2 caps at breakfast) really helped me recover from diarrhea after the OBMT.
2024 - 2025, off and on, 2 months at a time: BioMatrix Pylori-X (2 caps after breakfast and 2 caps after dinner) with Nature's Way DGL Ultra (1 cap after breakfast and 1 cap after dinner) – this definitely cleared up heartburn and acid feeling in stomach, although it came back when I stopped.
2024: Matula tea: May have helped somewhat, but I think I did it wrong and accidentally diluted it with water.
Eating more regularly (e.g. breakfast before 8, lunch around noon, and dinner before 7) seemed to help
Bloating:
Did SIBO protocol 3 months in summer 2023 - maybe helped with SIBO? but not bloating
Simple Carbohydrate Diet (no simple carbs/grains, dairy, etc)
Metagenics Candibactin BR - 1 cap 2x/day
Metagenics Candibactin AR - 1 cap 2x/day starting on day 8
Other things for bloating
PHP Fatty Acid Assist - 1 cap/day - I feel like this helped
Vagus nerve - opera style singing seemed to help, see note under fatigue about singing
Rotated foods on 4 day schedule (i.e. eat no ingredient more than 1x every 4 days) – didn't help
Eating less or fasting often helped
Rotated foods weekly (I.e. "A" weeks and "B" weeks) – seemed to help somewhat
Quell Shop Digest Aid – 1 cap before meals – did not help and immediately gave me a strange acidic/gassy feeling, especially around lunch or dinner
Jarrow bile acid factors – 1 - 2 caps after dinner – maybe helped somewhat
Nutricology Ox Bile 500 mg – 1 cap with breakfast, 1 cap with dinner – it definitely helped while I was taking it
June 2025: Procaine injections and ozone around belly button, gall bladder, sternum, and appendectomy scars, followed by Jarrow Shilajit 1 cap/day for 3 days – this helped, have barely had any bloating since
Fatigue:
Potassium iodide - 1 - 2 drops per day - I felt like this helped with energy… I often felt like I would start to move from fatigue to restlessness after a few weeks or months of 2 drops per day so I would cut back, although that could be a coincidence
Caffeine or chocolate - When I use these I feel great and get a surge of energy and creativity, although it's easy to overdo it and feel wired/manic/restless and then crash afterward
No screens or blue light 1+ hours before bed - Definitely helps
Waking up and seeing the sunrise - I feel great when I do this, but I have trouble getting to bed consistently, so I find that I'm not sleeping enough and revert back to sleeping in later.
Sleeping more - more sleep does help. For over a decade, it used to be that my body would not let me sleep past 7.5 hours no matter what time I went to sleep, and often if I tried to go back to sleep, I couldn't, or would get a headache. In the past half year or so, I have sometimes been sleeping longer than 7.5 hours, and usually have been able to go back to sleep very easily, so it seems like my body is wanting more sleep. The downside of getting more sleep is that my mood takes a hit (more depression and anxiety) and I seem to have less self-control.
Lifting weights and/or walking both help.
Rucking - this feels like a cheat code – it seems like every time I ruck, it permanently increases the amount of energy and strength I have available. I usually walk for 28 - 30 minutes. Haven't done it for a while though.
Vagus nerve - I did opera-style singing (had lessons in the past) for about 10 minutes every morning for much of 2023 and it seemed to help a lot with fatigue and brain fog. Also possibly with bloating though I can't say for sure.
Spending time with friends helps.
Seborrheic dermatitis:
At first I thought this was sunburn, then a naturopath suggested it was a sun reaction. I later saw a dermatologist in Oct 2023 who diagnosed it as seborrheic dermatitis. The two times in the past year that I've taken antibiotics (OBMT in July 2024 and Metranidazole for appendicitis in April 2025), seb derm symptoms got worse afterward.
I have tried:

Zinc oxide sunscreen, then for cleaning - Toups & Co Seabuckthorn cleansing oil + Toups & Co Facial Tonic (Calm): helped on the days I used them
Washing face with Essentials Unscented Glycerin soap bar: Doesn't seem to help
Washing face + hair with Unscented Company soap bar: Seems to help
Jojoba oil: Definitely helps – skin improves for a few days afterward
Quell Shop Daily Butter: Slightly helps
Ketoconazole shampoo: Maybe helped? Hard to tell
Ketoconazole cream: Maybe helped? Hard to tell, didn't do it as long as prescribed because it irritated my eyes
Fluconazole: 4 caps over 2 weeks – Helped, see note below about antifungal protocol
I currently have another Rx for fluconazole, and for Zoryve topical foam. I didn't start them yet because my liver enzymes were high.
Taking vitamin A (Pure Encapsulations 3000 mcg) - 1 cap daily - I feel like this probably helped.
"Anti-microbial protocol":
Worked with a clinical nutritionist who specializes in skin rashes. I did her 8-week cohort in Sept 2023, then had a 1-on-1 consultation with her in April 2024 and she gave me a specific protocol to follow that would address dysbiosis seen on my GI Map results, and also would address fungal overgrowth which she suspected due to seb derm and other symptoms. She also suspected I had SIBO. She initially recommended I do the GI doc's OBMT Rx, followed by fluconazole, then UVA ursi + olive leaf extract, then her own herbal antifungal protocol.
See notes above regarding OBMT and fluconazole. I did those before starting the protocols below.

UVA ursi + olive leaf extract: This made a noticeable improvement in just 10 days. I wish I remembered what symptom. I think it was either fatigue, or sleep.
Karuna Olive Leaf extract - 2 caps 3x a day with meals for 10 days
Nature's Way Uva Ursi standardized - 2 caps 3x a day with meals for 10 days
Antifungal protocol, did for 8 weeks. I started this immediately after fluconazole on recommendation of nutritionist. I did notice a difference in seb derm, not sure if it was fluconazole or this.
Solaray Monolaurin 500mg - 3x/day before meals
BioMatrix Candid-X - 2 caps 3x/day before meals, introduced slowly
I really respect this nutritionist's clinical experience and knowledge of scientific research but she tends to use a lot of fear-based messaging, which definitely caused extra stress.
——————————–
——————————–
Elimination diet:
In April 2023, a naturopath had me take 2 blood tests, one for food sensitivities, and one for food allergies. As a result I eliminated over 80 individual foods. The naturopath said that after 10 weeks I would start reintroducing them one by one; if they didn't affect me negatively I could keep them in my diet, and if they did, I would leave them out for another 10 weeks. The problem was, my symptoms were so inconsistent that I could never tell whether a food was causing the issue or if it was just my body acting up that day, so it took me forever to reintroduce these foods, and I still haven't reintroduced some of them. I don't remember seeing any difference at all when I cut out these foods. The only thing I noticed was a huge increase in stress because it was so hard to follow the diet.
(Prior to the elimination diet, I had a couple of times where I noticed a specific food was bothering me, and I cut it out for a few months and then reintroduced it, and that worked fine. So I don't object to cutting out foods strategically if needed.)
In general, fear is a very effective but destructive motivator for me. I tend to think of the worst case scenario, imagine how the worst case scenario will play out, and therefore be perfectionistic about a rule, usually at the expense of a healthy mindset/flow. With the elimination diet, when I failed at getting enough to eat, instead of relaxing the rules a bit, I just didn't eat and my caloric intake suffered.
I think what works better for me is understanding the big picture and having an ideal flow to aim for, as well as what to prioritize in situations where the ideal is not possible.

51. Are you currently taking any supplements? If so, please list them and be as specific as possible, including the brand and dosage of any supplement. Please mention how helpful (or unhelpful) you perceive them to be.
Yes
Quell Shop P2 Detox Balance - 3 caps with breakfast, 3 caps with dinner – I think this is generally helpful for liver which I think affects other areas including bloating
NOW Glycine powder - 6g at breakfast, 6g at dinner – same comment as P2 Liver Detox
Designs for Health Vitamin D Supreme (with Vitamin K2) - 1 cap every other day at breakfast - I had very low Vitamin D back in Dec 2020 (17ng/ml) so this helped with that, otherwise I don't notice much difference
Seeking Health Homocysteine Nutrients - 1 cap with breakfast, I don't notice much difference
Nature's Way Vitamin B1 - 1 cap with breakfast, I don't notice much difference
Nature's Way DGL Ultra - 1 cap with dinner, I think this in combination with Pylori-X helped with heartburn/H Pylori symptoms (I actually just stopped taking these last week)
Concentrace Trace Mineral drops - 1.5 drops per every 1 oz of water I drink - water without these drops tastes bitter and makes me feel nauseated, except for Fiji or other mineral water. I have typically used ProOne filters, but recently since moving I have been buying bottled water.
Spry xylitol gum - 1 piece after each meal, I don't notice much difference
Biocidin Botanicals Dentalmin Pro toothpaste - 1x per day - When I first started, teeth felt much cleaner… also I have been noticing my saliva smells different since using it
52. Are you currently taking any medications? If so, please list them and be as specific as possible, including the brand and dosage of any medication.
No
53. Are you currently diagnosed with any medical conditions? If so, please list them.
Yes
Seborrheic dermatitis.
PCP said I have high blood pressure, but this only seems to happen in their office, not other offices and not at home. And my last visit to PCP, bp was normal.
54. Please describe your diet. What foods do you typically eat, and in what amounts? Are these foods prepared by you, or are they pre-packaged, or from a restaurant? At what times do you typically eat?
I know a lot of this stuff is probably not great. Looking forward to learning how to do things better!

Overall, I have a lot of trouble getting enough calories to maintain healthy weight/energy levels. That's partly been due to bloating/inconsistent appetite (although both are much better lately) and partly due to planning, decision making, and confusion about nutrition.

Foods:
Organic, pasture-raised, grass fed whenever possible. Decent at avoiding seed oils (avoided them completely for 2023 and 2024, currently maybe 3 - 4x per month eating a serving of something with seed oils).

I've avoided eating added sugar for the past decade or two and as a result I seem to be somewhat sensitive when I do eat more than 7g at a time (stomach feels a bit sour). Also, over the past year or so, I've been eating fewer carbs because I was worried about my blood sugar levels (I now know I was mistaken about that linkage). I don't think I do very well on low carb diets, it's hard to get full and I have less energy, although it does seem to help sometimes with mental clarity. Recently I've been trying to increase my sugar and carb tolerance.

At breakfast I generally eat meat + fat only, sometimes with a low-carb vegetable.

At lunch and dinner I generally eat:

Meat (sometimes pre-packaged like Charcutnuvo sausages or Wild Planet canned chicken/salmon, sometimes Sprouts rotisserie chicken, sometimes chicken or beef I cooked myself)

Starch (like non-seed oil varieties of Amy's soup, or rice or potatoes I cooked myself, or butternut squash I steamed from frozen, or Siete cassava tortillas). Sometimes I don't have a starch with lunch.

A vegetable (like steamed veggies from frozen like broccoli, cauliflower, kale).

When I cook I usually use Chosen-brand avocado oil. I have in the past used Beef tallow and Duck fat.

In the past I added in calorie gaps with extra servings of EVOO + vinegar. Currently I do that with:

Potato chips (Siete or Vandy)

Tortilla chips (Siete or MASA)

Fresh avocado

Store-bought salsa

Epic meat bars

Pre-packaged bone broth

Crackers

Hummus

I used to eat pecans and pine nuts I roasted myself every day, but stopped since June due to possible kidney stress.

On weekends I often eat chocolate like unsweetened dark chocolate chips, Undercover chocolate quinoa crisps, or Honey Mama's chocolate snacks, and sometimes green or black decaf tea. I would eat chocolate every day if I could (I've read that heavy metals make that a bad idea).

Since April this year, cooking was difficult due to recovering from surgery, so I've been using a personal chef service for 10 meals per week. They use organic, pasture-raised, grass-fed groceries. Each meal includes a protein like chicken, pork, or beef; a roasted or sauteed vegetable or two; and usually a starch like rice, potato, etc. They use a lot of variety and flavor, but calories are very inconsistent and it's super expensive. Plus my strength is back for cooking, so I am planning to cancel.

Timing:
I usually eat breakfast between 8:30 and 10:0 a.m. I know I feel better (appetite and energy) when I eat earlier, but I don't usually get going early enough.

I eat lunch anywhere from 12 p.m. to 2:30 p.m., but usually around 12:30 or 1:00.

Dinner has typically been between 7:00 to 9:00 p.m. I think I do better when I eat earlier, like 5:30 or 6 pm, although I don't always have an appetite that early.

I generally try to consolidate eating around mealtimes (i.e., no snacks). If I have snack foods I want to eat like chocolate, I try to eat them with or immediately after meals. The exception is if I get to the end of the day and I haven't eaten enough, sometimes I'll have an Epic bar or nuts before going to bed.

I tend to be a very slow eater, so if a meal has a big chunk of slightly dry or tough meat like chicken breast or steak, or difficult to eat veggies like brussels sprouts or roasted broccolini, it's not enjoyable for me to eat and it's stressful, so I tend to start watching screens which makes the meal take even longer. But if it's very tender or is in stew or soup form, I do much better with that.

My teeth have been drifting since having braces in childhood. Wearing my retainer helps, but tends to cause neck tension. When I don't wear it, my bottom teeth start to hit my top teeth which makes it difficult to eat. I do think that if this weren't an issue, I could eat more quickly and with less stress.

55. Please describe your lifestyle. Do you get outside often? Do you exercise? How stressful is your day-to-day life due to work or other factors?
I usually take a 20 - 40min walk outside after waking up (UVA), and sometimes a 20 min walk outside after dinner. I sometimes get about 15 - 20 minutes of UVB light, either going for a walk at lunch or running errands.

I typically total between 4000 and 11000 steps per day.

I go hiking for 30 - 60 min maybe once a month. If I could do this every day, I would. In Texas this can be difficult, due to the heat, especially when I'm not getting enough calories.

I am doing a kettlebell + heavy club swinging program 5 days per week. Very light weights since surgery. 4-week program starts at 15 min per day and increases to 40 min per day, then repeats with a heavier weight. (I'm increasing weights faster to get back up to normal.)

I used to live in California, both in Silicon Valley and in San Diego. I spent a ton of time hiking in the foothills of the Santa Cruz mountains and in the coastal cliffs of La Jolla plus swimming in the ocean. I would love to get back to that kind of thing. I'm in DFW so I'm landlocked and the heat makes hiking less of a recreation/relaxing/stress-relieving activity and more of a strenuous exercise. I also don't like wearing sunscreen. I know theoretically how to build up my tolerance for sunlight, but kind of got off track this spring due to the surgery episode and tend to err on the side of not going outside to avoid getting burned.

Work is stressful, not that I'm working overtime, but because I'm a fundamentally creative person in an industry (software development) that drains creative energy without allowing for creative freedom or creative fulfillment. I'm working on building my own business which will hopefully help with that. Also working with a career advisor to possibly find another position in the meantime.

I generally find it difficult to relax. This could be partly due to screen addiction, partly due to feeling like I don't have freedom to be doing what I'm supposed to be doing (creating), and partly because I feel like I have a lot of health issues hanging over my head, and no current plan to address them.

I've developed some germophobic and hypochondriac tendencies. Partly due to past experiences, partly due to incomplete info. I tend to catastrophize which adds stress.

Going to sleep is usually not so much a problem physically (unless I didn't get enough movement or the room is too hot), but mentally, I find it very difficult to wind down, especially when I'm worrying about health or work.

My experience in the past has been that proactively taking ownership over my health has definitely helped with my stress levels and ability to relax, which is why I'm working with Prism.

I moved to Texas in 2009 to join a church which I later realized was a cult. I finally left in 2020. I developed some very unhealthy ways of relating, thinking, and responding to stress which I've been healing from. I've come a long way, still have some way to go. Therapy has helped (I still meet with a counselor occasionally).

Due to the cult, I lost a lot of my friend group and gained trust issues, so it's been difficult building a new group of friends. I have a few, but contact is limited. I'm considering moving to Arizona to be near family.

56. What are your health primary goals you are looking achieve?
A big motivator for me is inspiring people through the use of writing, storytelling, worldbuilding, music, and coaching. I'm not 100% clear on what it will look like and I want the freedom to explore the ideas I've already started.

Being an inspiration to other people means my own inspiration tank needs to be full. Adventures, taking in beauty and story, spending time with people, and unbounded time to let the mind wander and imagine are key.

My big picture goals are:

Not be in a nursing home when I'm 90
Transform my mindset toward health so I spend less time worrying and needing reassurance from doctors
Reintroduce healthy foods (dairy, eggs, shellfish, gluten) so I can travel and eat with friends
Make eating (and planning for meals) enjoyable again, not a source of stress
Have enough energy, strength, and GI resilience that I can have adventures
Long term adventure goals:
Hike the Inca Trail

Visit Greece/Italy and eat the local food

Learn to sail

Learn to surf

Attend a Wildman Athletica adventure retreat for kettlebell/heavy club/staff fighting

Be able to swing a kettlebell or heavy club for 10 minutes

Intermediate adventure goals:
Attend a US-based Wildman Athletica kettlebell/heavy club seminar

Learn to fight with a staff

Go bouldering 2x/week

Play racquetball or pickleball regularly with friends

Go on regular day hikes

Do a 1-week cabin retreat

Drive to Phoenix

Take a beach vacation

Have enough strength and resilience that I can help people in a disaster scenario (or just help them move)
Be able to work on creative projects (e.g. playing & performing guitar/piano, writing, typing/coding) as much as I want to, which requires:
Mental clarity

Joint resilience (meaning carpal tunnel syndrome isn't an issue)

Time (meaning health is low maintenance enough)

Low enough overall stress levels that inspiration can bubble up

Clear up seborrheic dermatitis (it's high maintenance, irritates my eyes and is socially limiting)
Reduce floaters/eye strain (I'm also working through an endmyopia.org course)
Be able to spend lots of time outdoors without getting sunburned
57. Have you ever had food poisoning before? If so, when?
Not sure. I had a couple times in 2021/2 when I had a 5-day bout with diarrhea, not sure if that was food poisoning. Prior to that, I don't think I ever did, except maybe for a single night when I was 10 years old.

58. Please list your height, weight and waist measurements.
Height: 5'9"

Weight: 123 lb

Waist: 40 cm

59. Please list any other information you think would help us help you.
I tend to be super aware of sensations and things going on in my body. A lot of times I can't tell if something warrants concern. As a result I tend to worry a lot and have a fear-based, reactive approach to health, which hasn't worked very well for me. I'd like to learn how to have a more balanced, calm approach, and know when something needs attention vs when my body's natural self-healing faculties will be enough.

Have also developed some OCD-ish germophobic and hypochondriac tendencies. Some of this is a result of past experiences, and some if it is probably due to bad info or missing context, including taking medical/product warning labels too literally. I think what would help with this is absorbing a healthy mindset from someone who is in the know.

In May I moved out of an apartment that had visible mold in the shower and HVAC duct. I lived there from June 2021 - May 2025. The clinical nutritionist suggested that living with mold was preventing me from recovering from H Pylori and fungal overgrowth.

Had asthma/environmental allergies as a kid. Neither bother me as much as they used to, at least not with respiratory symptoms. An allergist in Nov 2023 said my body had an extremely strong reaction to dust mites in a prick test and recommended I take antihistamines regularly and clean more often. I haven't done either. Maybe this is affecting me more than I realize?

In Nov 2023 the allergist also did a prick test for several foods that I hadn't been eating. A few foods did show a skin reaction. The allergist said I didn't have any life-threatening allergies and suggested I try reintroducing those foods. I haven't fully done it yet.

As a kid I used to get debilitating intestinal spasms/cramps a lot. In college I started drinking more water and they stopped. I do think they were sometimes linked to stress and maybe sometimes to certain foods. I've had them recently a few times in the last couple years.

In Feb 2020, I had an episode where I had mysterious strong pain in my entire tongue for 2 weeks. Later the lymph node under my jaw appeared to be enlarged, but an ENT ordered a CT scan and said it was just scarring around my saliva gland and suggested I may have had a saliva stone or some other past "inflammation".

A couple years ago (2023 or so), I used to experience trembling in my hands when trying to perform slow controlled movements. I was eating a lot of canned fish and was concerned about mercury so I decreased the amount of fish I was eating. Things got better, but it could have been a coincidence.

I occasionally experience trembling in my chest/neck muscles when breathing deeply.

I mentioned nightmares – these only seem to happen (and happen fairly consistently) when I'm dehydrated, but it seems like I have to drink a ton of water to avoid them, which causes me to wake up to urinate.

My mom has had a lot of gut issues since at least the time I was in high school and recently discovered she had thyroid issues. My sister was diagnosed with Hashimoto's in the past 5 years or so.

The past couple of winters, my gut has been really sensitive to cold weather and cold liquids. My gut area would feel cold and cramped/spasming, I would get bloated, and I would have to bundle up, wear thick socks, or drink hot liquids to mitigate it.

Over the past year or two I noticed wounds were healing very slowly. After taking Vitamin A for a few months this seemed to get better.

In June 2025, I had been occasionally noticing mild pain in kidney area. When a naturopath did a routine Zyto feedback scan, kidney stress showed up as super high. She gave me a kidney cleanse to do but I haven't done it yet. I did reduce red meat and nuts. I haven't had kidney pain for about a month. It's possible the pain was due more to my bed being on an incline causing my back muscles to tense up.

In August 2023, due to a Zyto scan a naturopath had me do a test for Lyme disease. The test came back with numbers low enough for the lab to report a negative result, but high enough that the naturopath wanted to treat it (we never got around to that). I did have a possible tick bite in 2018 or 2019. More recent Zyto scans also showed Lyme as a stressor. Not sure how useful those scans are.

Appointment #1
With: Shannon Sirois 
Billing code: 
consulting
Edit
4:00 PM – 5:00 PM
Progress Note 
***his labs that say hospital was when he had acute appendicitis***

William is a 41 y.o. computer programmer that moved to Texas from So California (sounds like maybe during the pandemic) and is now residing there in the Dallas area. He is very dialed into health, and after reading his intake paperwork I was a bit concerned that he would be one of those high maintenance clients, but luckily his journey has lead him to a place where his gut is generally good, he just wants help with diet, weight gain and overall vitality.

His main goals:
Nutrition (increase calories, would like to use coffee, return to eating dairy and fruit)

Sleep (wake up refreshed)

Since his intake was extremely thorough, there’s not much I need to say. The things we focused on were how to help him get in more caloric load, why his appetite gets suppressed, his goals with Prism and how he can navigate finding a career that aligns with his values.

Nutrition: he’d like guidance on the best things to eat and how to increase his caloric load again after all he’s been through without causing a relapse of sx’s. Recent procain injections in his abdomen, diaphragm, etc have made a marked difference in his digestion. It sounds like bouts of appendicitis, abx and an eventual appendectomy put him in a poor state of gut health. He has sought out many strategies that have helped this, with the most recent one being visceral manipulation and these procain injections that finally stopped his bloating. Now he’d like to gain weight from where he is at 123 lbs. He would like to get to 150 lbs.

Sleep: He wasn’t really sure where this went wrong, he said that he used to sleep very soundly and always wake up rather early and refreshed. Over the past few years, he’s admittedly been addicted to screen use so he’s not sure if this has affected it, but he goes to be later than he should and instead of waking up refreshed and ready to go, he feels like he’s groggy and still tired and could sleep much longer. His current routine is 11pm-8am, but he would prefer to be up earlier than this if possible. We did discuss his current situation for feeling “stuck”. He does not enjoy his work, but feels too overwhelmed to do something on his own and make the move. He said this is a huge demotivator for him and often why he loses himself in research and other things that have him on his phone/computer a lot of the time in addition to his regular job.

Strategies we talked about: Eat breakfast within 30 min of waking, so he’s going to start eating around 8 instead of waiting an hour and over time slowly titrating calories up. I will calculate the caloric load he will need to get to in order to gain 25 lbs and I will work him through reverse dieting essentially, until he’s at that caloric spot. I’m going to start with dairy reintroduction with real, whole mozzarella cheese. If he tolerates this, then next will be cream or cream cheese. If we can get a decent amount of dairy in his diet without ill effects, next will be fruit.

Strategies for sleep: screen time is an obvious one, but he said that his mind races and that’s why he chooses screens to distract himself. I am going to send him a video for a breathing exercise to do before bed. This is a box breathing technique, it’s simple and you just lie down with your feet up on something and reset your nervous system. I’m really hoping that bumping up his calories is going to help him sleep deeper and wake up more refreshed. I explained to him that upping his b vitamin intake and absorption will likely improve his caffeine tolerance, as he would love to return to drinking coffee again. I think this may help give him something to look forward to in the morning. He didn’t really connect with this when I asked him, but I get the impression that his work is soul-sucking and I know when I was in that position, I certainly did not wake up wanting to tackle my day either!!

His current supplement stack:

Quell Shop P2 Detox Balance - 3 caps with breakfast, 3 caps with dinner – I think this is generally helpful for liver which I think affects other areas including bloating

NOW Glycine powder - 6g at breakfast, 6g at dinner – same comment as P2 Liver Detox

Designs for Health Vitamin D Supreme (with Vitamin K2) - 1 cap every other day at breakfast - I had very low Vitamin D back in Dec 2020 (17ng/ml) so this helped with that, otherwise I don't notice much difference

Seeking Health Homocysteine Nutrients - 1 cap with breakfast, I don't notice much difference

Nature's Way Vitamin B1 - 1 cap with breakfast, I don't notice much difference

Nature's Way DGL Ultra - 1 cap with dinner, I think this in combination with Pylori-X helped with heartburn/H Pylori symptoms (I actually just stopped taking these last week)

Concentrace Trace Mineral drops - 1.5 drops per every 1 oz of water I drink - water without these drops tastes bitter and makes me feel nauseated, except for Fiji or other mineral water. I have typically used ProOne filters, but recently since moving I have been buying bottled water.

Spry xylitol gum - 1 piece after each meal, I don't notice much difference

Biocidin Botanicals Dentalmin Pro toothpaste - 1x per day - When I first started, teeth felt much cleaner… also I have been noticing my saliva smells different since using it

I’m not going to copy all of his intake, it was extensive! Give it a quick scan, but the TLDR is that he’s done it all. SIBO protocol, fungal protocols, treatments for seborrheic dermatitis, low fodmap, no gluten/dairy/eggs, etc. There’s not much he hasn’t done! He finally achieved decreased bloating, but it sounds like his digestion may need a bit of a boost in order to get him to a place where he can tolerate increasing his calories.

Plan:

Macros: Current ratios at 123 lbs will be 150 g protein, 200 grams carbs and 70 grams of fat for 2130 total calories. Goal is to get to 2,500 calories with a weight gain of 25 lbs and maintain this new weight. I will slowly titrate him up over time, and once I know his digestion can handle it, I will throw in bulk weeks for him, but the goal will be gaining lean mass. Each week I will add 50-60 calories in the form of 12-15 grams of carbs, or 5-6 grams of fat, depending on how he’s doing.

Digestive enzyme: I prefer masszymes, but am open to others if you have thoughts!

Triphala: Vitamin C (he’s not getting much) and for constipation

Let’s give him that recent tributyrin Collin linked in Discord! That looks awesome and I’ve been having really good luck with both tributyrin and butyrate.

B vitamins: He’s taking shitting B1, so let’s get him on a more comprehensive b vitamin

Forefront health liquid b’s here.

Zinc carnosine: for stomach acid and nutrient replenishment

I sent him a book about screen addiction and box breathing exercises already. He’s continuing with visceral manip for his upper quadrant pain so he’s good here.

I’m at a loss for what else to do here. He’s done SO much, I really feel like this is a case where good digestion is needed so he can bump his calories up. This is going to be life-changing for him. I’m open to any other ideas! His temps weren’t terrible and other than recent slight constipation, I don’t get the sense he’s in dire need of thyroid. If you guys think he need anything for hormones, please step in!

Labs? Would you guys like some labs on him? We didn’t even really get there because we were more focused on his food/nutrition and planning his escape from the shackles of corporate America but if we feel like he’s benefit, I get the impression he’s open to it!
